excerpts.query: main switch inverter on off charger operation
excerpts.text:
MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual   
25

Event commands 
When the MLI Ultra is configured as an event target by another device, this device can initiate an 
event command and an event action to be performed by the MLI Ultra. 
Event command Meaning 
Relay open The battery safety relay will be opened. 
Relay close The battery safety relay will be closed.

Stop charge event 
Before the battery reaches the limits of the operating specifications, the Stop Charge event is 
triggered. When configured (see page 15), this event forces the Mastervolt charger to switch off and 
resume charging after 10 minutes. The settings depend on the used charger because different 
chargers have different state options. Check the manual of the used charger for how to switch the 
charging state. 
Battery safety event 
PLEASE CAREFULLY READ THE FOLLOWING INFORMATION! 
A battery safety event triggers the safety relay to automatically disconnect the battery from the loads 
and charging devices. There are two kinds of battery safety events: a resettable and a non-
resettable.  
• Resettable battery safety event 
A resettable safety event occurs if the internal temperature of on

excerpts.query: DC fuse battery protection wiring supply requirement
excerpts.text:
MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual   
13

It may be that the safety relay is mounted on top of the MLI Ultra. For example with an Ultra 
Connection Kit (product code 79007712 or 79007724), or as part of a Soft Start Module (SSM). The 
SSM is only used in 48V systems, where it is occasionally active to guarantee the lifespan of the 
safety relay. 
Installation procedure for a single unit

WARNING! 
Always comply with all local rules and regulations.

WARNING! 
Do not use a sensor for temperature compensated charging. Temperatures of the 
battery and its cells are monitored by the built-in BMS. 
1. Switch off all loads and chargers. 
2. Tilt the side handles to uncover the battery terminals.

3. Integrate a fuse holder in the positive battery wire but do not place the fuse yet. 
4. Set the safety relay knob to “LOCK OFF”, see page 12.  
5. Integrate the safety relay in the positive battery wire. 
Disconnect the battery safety connector, item (6) in figure Main Parts on page 8, by loosening 
its screws. Connect the wires of the safety relay onto the battery safety connector as shown in 
the installation diagram on page 11 (see also chapter 16 in case of
excerpts.source_heading_guess: 1. Switch off all loads and chargers.

excerpts.query: DC fuse battery protection wiring supply requirement
excerpts.text:
34   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual

CAUTION! 
Only create multiple battery systems with batteries of the same type and capacity.  
 Make sure that all batteries are fully charged. 
 In case of replacing, make sure that the batteries have been charged and have the same 
product codes. 
 If connected in series, then two units can be protected by one safety relay. 
If connected in parallel, then each unit requires its own safety relay! 
 Use properly sized fuses and wiring. 
Note: Keep the various interconnect cables as short as possible and the same length. As you 
can see in the following figures, all short cables connecting the MLI Ultra units together, 
and all long cables are of equal length. The cables in question are marked by one or more 
slashes.
excerpts.source_heading_guess: 34   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual

excerpts.query: BMS battery safety relay protective disconnect undervoltage overvoltage temperature
excerpts.text:
44   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual

MLI Ultra 12/3000 MLI Ultra 12/6000 MLI Ultra 24/6000 
Chemistry Lithium Iron Phosphate 
Interfacing   
MasterBus connectivity Yes 
Powering capabilities for 
MasterBus 
Yes 
CZone Yes 
External disconnect switch Yes, MANDATORY, direct connection for safety relay 
Important available monitoring/events 
Battery Safety event  Preventing unsafe overcharge, over discharge and 
overtemperature 
Stop Charge event Communication to charging devices preventing overcharging 
1) If a Bulk/Absorption voltage of 14V is used (User Defined battery type), the Stop Charge Event is not required. 
2) Charging at temperatures below -5ºC / 23ºF may result in battery not accepting charge current . 
3) Temperatures below 5ºC / 41ºF and above 25ºC / 77ºF may affect lifetime and cycle life. See page 45. 
4) Trigger point for battery safety event.

excerpts.query: Close relay reset battery safety event recovery after disconnect
excerpts.text:
26   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual

• Non-resettable battery safety event 
A non-resettable safety event occurs if the internal temperature of one of the individual cells 
exceeds 75°C / 167°F, or if the voltage level exceeds the major minimum or maximum levels. See 
chapter 18 for details.

WARNING! 
IF A NON-RESETTABLE BATTERY SAFETY EVENT OCCURS: SET 
THE SAFETY RELAY(S) TO “LOCK OFF”, SEE PAGE 12, AND 
CONTACT A MASTERVOLT REPRESENTATIVE FOR ASSISTANCE. 
How to activate MasterBus powering 
1. Remove the DIP switch cover by loosening the four screws. 
2. Use a small screwdriver to carefully set the DIP switches: 
• Set DIP switch 1 ON to select MasterBus. 
• Set DIP switch 2 ON to set MasterBus powering to Automatic. 
The following picture is only an example!

3. Replace the DIP switch cover and fasten with the screws. Make sure that the rubber band is 
placed correctly, to make it watertight. 
In most cases this is the desired setting. MasterBus powering can also be set to Always on. This 
can only be selected in MasterAdjust. See MB power in section Configuration on page 20. 
.
excerpts.source_heading_guess: 26   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual

40   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual

6. Click the Confirm topology button.

Notice the status of the cluster topology. It can be: 
- Approved; 
- Needs approval: the topology was not confirmed; 
- Not activated: the topology still requires activation; 
- Too many devices/Double device/Incomplete/Missing self: the 
proposed topology is not possible or other units need to be 
selected.  
After confirmation, the cluster topology is defined and fields to 
select the other units will appear. Note that S01-P01 is pre-filled 
with this unit but can be changed later.

7. In the S01 field, from the drop-down list, select the serial number 
of the Primary battery. This battery will be the Cluster Primary 
battery and will show the Cluster state. 
8. In the following fields, select the other clustered batteries. 
9. Click the Activate button. 
Now, each battery will show its individual data in the monitoring 
tab while the Primary battery will show both the individual data 
and the data of the cluster.  
Activation will fail if the battery is already in another cluster.

10. If required, select to base the displayed SOC on the lowest 
(minimum) instead of t

6   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual

2. LIABILITY 
Mastervolt cannot be held liable for:  
- Consequential damage resulting from the use of the MLI Ultra. 
- Possible errors in the included manual and the consequences of these.  
- Use that is inconsistent with the purpose of the MLI Ultra. 
Disclaimer:  Our products are subject to continual development and improvement. Therefore, 
additions or modifications to the products may cause changes to the technical data and functional 
specifications. No rights can be derived from this document. Please consult our online Terms & 
Conditions of Sale. 
3. WARRANTY 
The Mastervolt product warranty covers the MLI Ultra for the first two years after the purchase date, 
on the condition that the product is installed and used according to the instructions in this manual. 
Installation or use that does not comply with these instructions may result in under performance, 
damage or failure of the product and may void this warranty. The warranty is limited to the cost of 
repair and/or replacement of the product. Costs of labor or shipping are not covered by this 
warranty. 
4. CYCLE LIFE AND C-RATE 
The C-rate ind

excerpts.text:
To access the individual batteries, unfold the cluster (i.e. click the plus sign) in the left panel of 
MasterAdjust. 
To identify the Primary battery, go to the configuration page of one of the (other) batteries. 
To remove a battery from a cluster, click the Detach button on the Configuration tab of the battery in 
question. 
To breakdown a cluster, in the configuration of the Primary battery: set the cluster to 1 (series) by 1 
(parallel) and click the Confirm topology button.

excerpts.text:
MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 – User and Installation Manual   
41

How to create a cluster in CZone 
Preconditions: - a CZone system is already configured, including all batteries; 
 - the CZone Configuration Tool opened the required configuration file. 
1. In the CZone Configuration Tool, in the Modules tab, double-click the MLI Ultra that will act as 
Primary battery. 
2. The Module Modifications window appears. Click the Advanced Settings button. 
3. From the Cluster Type drop-down list, 
select Cluster Primary. 
4. Cluster DC Instance is an NMEA2000 
instance that is used to differentiate 
between multiple monitoring sources. 
5. Enter a Cluster Name. 
6. If the cluster is a series connection, tick Set 
Relay type of all secondary batteries to 
Daisy Chain. 
7. Click the Configure Topology button. 
The Configured Battery Cluster Topology 
window appears. 
8. Enter the number of batteries in series (S). 
If there are no batteries in series, enter 1. 
9. Enter the number of batteries in parallel (P). 
If there are no batteries in parallel, enter 1. 
10. To activate voltage balancing of all cells in 
series, select ON from the Series Balancing 
drop-down list. 
11. Click th

assembled_user_prompt:
You extract an interaction profile for one piece of marine equipment from excerpts
of its official manual(s). You record observable facts about how humans interact
with the device. You do NOT judge importance or guide tier. You do NOT write guest
prose.

Rules:

1. Use ONLY the manual text provided. No general knowledge about this product or
   brand. Unmentioned = empty array / false / "unclear". Never guess.
   device.category_freeform must be the manual's own product category words
   (e.g. "charger/inverter", "MPPT solar charger") — never registry/taxonomy
   tokens such as electrical_dc, propulsion, freshwater_system.

2. Empty is meaningful. A busbar has no control surfaces and no operator actions;
   emit empty arrays.

3. audience per action, by the manual's own framing:
   - routine steps addressed to "you" → operator
   - "consult your dealer", "qualified personnel", installation-chapter procedures,
     DIP-switch / DIP table configuration → installer_or_technician
     (DIP configuration is always installer_or_technician + commissioning)
   - genuinely both → either

4. context per action: daily / situational / emergency / commissioning /
   maintenance. One procedure fitting two contexts → two entries.

5. Split actions: each action string is one verb phrase with one context.
   "monitor and configure via the app" → two actions (e.g. monitor → daily;
   configure → situational or commissioning per the manual's framing).
   Splitting bundled actions must never remove actions. Every distinct
   procedure the manual addresses to the operator must appear, including
   shutdown, restart, and fault/error-code procedures.
   has_emergency_procedure: true when the manual has an error-code / fault
   table or protective-disconnect recovery — not merely because an orderly
   service/storage shutdown exists (see calibration J).

6. control_surfaces on the base product vs. accessories sold separately: mark the
   latter optional_accessory: true. If a capability requires another product, also
   add a requires_devices entry with needed_for pointing at that field path
   (e.g. "control_surfaces[0]" or "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network").
   Never set needed_for to "" / null. Never attach requires_devices to a surface
   with optional_accessory: false (built-in capabilities do not "require" an
   accessory). Multiple requires_devices entries that share the same needed_for
   are OR alternatives (any one present on the vessel activates the capability);
   prefer separate entries per alternative product rather than stuffing "A or B"
   into one description when both are first-class options.

7. networks: record names verbatim in speaks (with physical_or_wireless when known).
   Ports/protocols that exist on the device belong in speaks even when their
   usefulness depends on another product — express that dependency with
   requires_devices, not by omitting the speak or zeroing the capability.
   Record a bridge ONLY if the manual explicitly describes translating/gatewaying
   between two named networks. Being connectable to a network is speaks, not bridges.

8. Multiple model variants in one manual: profile the requested model only. Ignore
   accessories and requirements that apply only to other variants. If the manual
   does not distinguish, say so in confidence.notes and lower confidence.overall.

9. evidence: 1-8 objects {supports_field, manual_section, note}. supports_field is
   the schema path justified. manual_section is a section title/heading (verbatim
   titles are expected). note is a <=12-word paraphrase — never copy manual
   sentences. Prefer coverage across consequential fields — not multiple evidence
   rows for one field. Fill in this priority order (stop when the cap is reached):
   (a) data_roles fields set true, and each requires_devices entry;
   (b) safety_role fields set true;
   (c) representative operator_actions (at least the emergency-context ones);
   (d) control_surfaces.
   Do not invent extra mandatory evidence beyond what this priority covers.

10. Optional protected_by / protects / supply_requirements: record when the
    manual explicitly states guard devices or supply needs — including in
    installer-framed installation chapters. audience applies to
    operator_actions only, never to requirement fields. Otherwise empty arrays.

11. safety_role keys are exactly: is_protective_device, has_manual_override,
    has_emergency_procedure. Do not invent other keys.

CALIBRATION — these examples concern OTHER devices / situations. Match the
field decisions (context, audience, optional_accessory, dependency shape).
Do NOT copy action strings or product-specific wording from the examples into
the device under extraction unless that wording appears in THIS device's
manual excerpts.

A. "To connect shore power: set the AC input current limit to match the dock
   supply using the rotary selector."
   → context: situational; audience: operator; one verb-phrase action naming
     the current-limit adjustment (word it from the manual under extraction).

B. "Battery type selection must be performed at commissioning. Consult your
   Balmar dealer for programming assistance."
   → context: commissioning; audience: installer_or_technician; one action for
     the battery-type selection (word it from the manual under extraction).

C. "If the BMS has disconnected the battery, remove the fault cause, then press
   and hold the reset button for 5 seconds."
   → context: emergency; audience: operator; one action describing that device's
     protective-disconnect recovery in the manual's own words (never paste a
     stock phrase from these examples).

D. "The charger can be monitored and configured via the VictronConnect app over
   Bluetooth."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, location_class: wireless,
     optional_accessory: false;
     PLUS two operator_actions: "monitor charger via VictronConnect app"
     (daily) and "configure charger via VictronConnect app" (situational or
     commissioning per framing) — never one combined "monitor and configure" action.

E. "With the optional Bluetooth Gateway, settings can be adjusted from the
   smartphone app."
   → control_surface: mobile_app_bluetooth, optional_accessory: true;
     plus requires_devices entry for the gateway with
     needed_for: "control_surfaces[0]".

F. "The unit connects to the NMEA 2000 backbone and transmits battery status PGNs."
   → networks.speaks: "NMEA 2000"; data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     bridges: EMPTY.

G. "The interface links the MasterBus network to CZone, allowing CZone displays
   to show MasterBus device data."
   → networks.bridges: [{from: "MasterBus", to: "CZone"}].

H. "Connect the unit's VE.Direct port to a GX device to monitor it remotely via VRM."
   → networks.speaks includes "VE.Direct" (wired);
     data_roles.exposes_data_to_network: true;
     requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "GX device",
     needed_for: "data_roles.exposes_data_to_network"}.
   Rationale: the port exists on the device → speaks; usefulness depending on
   another product → requires_devices, not by zeroing the capability or omitting
   the speak.

I. "BlueSolar models without built-in Bluetooth require the VE.Direct Bluetooth
   Smart Dongle." (when profiling a SmartSolar with built-in Bluetooth)
   → NO requires_devices entry for the dongle and NO control_surface change.
   Accessories that apply only to other model variants are ignored (see rule 8).

J. "To shut down the charger for service or storage: disconnect the PV supply,
   then the battery supply."
   → action: "shutdown the solar charger" (or equivalent); context: situational
   (or maintenance), NOT emergency. Prescribed orderly shutdown for service/
   storage is not an emergency procedure; fault response (error codes,
   protective disconnect recovery) is.

K. "The unit can be operated with the optional MasterView remote panel, installed
   at a convenient location."
   → control_surface: {surface: remote_panel_accessory, location_class:
     remote_wired, optional_accessory: true, label_verbatim from the manual};
     PLUS requires_devices: {description_verbatim: "MasterView remote panel"
     (or the manual's own panel name), needed_for: that surface's path}.
   Rationale: a separately sold panel/display that operates THIS device is a
   control surface of this device, not a different product.

L. "Install a fuse in the positive DC supply cable within 30cm of the battery."
   → supply_requirements entry describing that fuse mandate (word it from the
     manual under extraction — never paste this example sentence; include
     manual_section on the supporting evidence); also record protected_by when
     the manual frames the fuse as protecting this unit.
   Rationale: protection and supply mandates are FACTS to record even when the
   surrounding procedure is installer-audience — audience applies to
   operator_actions, never to requirement fields
   (supply_requirements / protected_by / protects).

M. (automatic protection + manual override — lithium/BMS style)
   Part 1 — Manual: "The safety relay will automatically open (REMOTE OFF) when
   built-in thresholds are met."
   → protects[] entry worded from the manual (safety relay automatically opens /
     protective disconnect on built-in thresholds). NOT an operator_action.
   Part 2 — Manual: "LOCK OFF knob position mechanically opens the safety relay"
     (or equivalent LOCK OFF / servicing isolation wording).
   → control_surfaces: {surface: rotary_selector (or physical_controls),
     location_class: on_device, optional_accessory: false, label from the manual};
     safety_role.has_manual_override: true.
   Operator recovery after a protective trip ("Close relay" once limits are OK)
   stays operator_actions with context: emergency — separate from Part 1/2.

Return JSON only — no markdown wrapper — matching the interaction_profile schema.

These excerpts are one part of a larger manual; profile only what THIS text supports; empty fields are correct if this text doesn't cover them.
(map group: batch_3)

    "text": "34   MLI Ultra 12/3000, 12/6000, 24/6000 \u2013 User and Installation Manual\n\nCAUTION! \nOnly create multiple battery systems with batteries of the same type and capacity.  \n Make sure that all batteries are fully charged. \n In case of replacing, make sure that the batteries have been charged and have the same \nproduct codes. \n If connected in series, then two units can be protected by one safety relay. \nIf connected in parallel, then each unit requires its own safety relay! \n Use properly sized fuses and wiring. \nNote: Keep the various interconnect cables as short as possible and the same length.
